{"fixed_xml":"<ITEM>Die Cramerischen Herren Erben und Gebrüder Philibert, Buchführere in Genff, proponiren dem Publico Subscriptions-Weis, eine neue Edition von <BIBL><AUTHOR>M. T. Ciceronis</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Operibus</TITLE>, cum Delectu Commentariorum per <AUTHOR>Josepho d'Olivet</AUTHOR>, in schön Pappier mit neuer Schrifft, von St. Augustini Text und Garamondi Noten; in conformität der Pariser Edition sehr correct heraus zugeben, und versprechen solches von allen darinn sich befindlichen Fehleren zu reinigen etc. das gantze Werck, welches in <FORMAT>4to</FORMAT> <VOLUME>9. Volumina</VOLUME> haltet, solle die Herren Praenumeranten nicht mehr als <PRICE>67 Livres Frantzösische Valuta</PRICE> oder <PRICE>40. Francken Genffer-Geld</PRICE> kosten etc. das weitere kan aus dem Project und Aviso bey Herrn Onoffrio Hoffmann allhier ersehen werden.</BIBL></ITEM>","number_of_corrections":11,"explanation":"Corrections made:
1. Fixed garbled character "Gebr√Ñ¬°der" to "Gebrüder"
2. Fixed garbled character "Buchf√Ñ¬°hrere" to "Buchführere"
3. Added missing space between "Publico" and "Subscriptions-Weis"
4. Fixed garbled "M. T.Ciccronis" to "M. T. Ciceronis" (added space, corrected spelling)
5. Fixed garbled "√Ñ¬†" (likely representing "per" or similar connector) between "Commentariorum" and "Josepho"
6. Fixed "Josepho dOlivet" to "Josepho d'Olivet" (added apostrophe)
7. Fixed garbled "sch√Ñ¬®n" to "schön"
8. Fixed garbled "conformit√Ñ¬§t" to "conformität"
9. Fixed garbled "√çƒ∆Æc" to "etc" (appears twice - Latin abbreviation for "et cetera")
10. Fixed mismatched tags: opening tags were <PRIZE> but closing tags were </PRICE> - standardized to <PRICE> for both instances
11. Fixed garbled "4t√Ñ¬§" in FORMAT tag to "4to" (standard Latin format abbreviation) and "Frantz√Ñ¬®sische" to "Frantzösische"

The XML structure itself was well-formed (proper nesting of ITEM > BIBL > AUTHOR/TITLE/FORMAT/VOLUME/PRICE tags), but contained character encoding corruption throughout (likely from a failed UTF-8/Latin-1 conversion) and a tag name mismatch between PRIZE (opening) and PRICE (closing)."}

Francken Genffer-Geld</PRICE> kosten etc. das weitere kan aus dem Project und Aviso bey Herrn Onoffrio Hoffmann allhier ersehen werden.</BIBL></ITEM>
parsed.explanation:
Corrections made:
1. Fixed garbled character "Gebr√Ñ¬°der" to "Gebrüder"
2. Fixed garbled character "Buchf√Ñ¬°hrere" to "Buchführere"
3. Added missing space between "Publico" and "Subscriptions-Weis"
4. Fixed garbled "M. T.Ciccronis" to "M. T. Ciceronis" (added space, corrected spelling)
5. Fixed garbled "√Ñ¬†" (likely representing "per" or similar connector) between "Commentariorum" and "Josepho"
6. Fixed "Josepho dOlivet" to "Josepho d'Olivet" (added apostrophe)
7. Fixed garbled "sch√Ñ¬®n" to "schön"
8. Fixed garbled "conformit√Ñ¬§t" to "conformität"
9. Fixed garbled "√çƒ∆Æc" to "etc" (appears twice - Latin abbreviation for "et cetera")
10. Fixed mismatched tags: opening tags were <PRIZE> but closing tags were </PRICE> - standardized to <PRICE> for both instances
11. Fixed garbled "4t√Ñ¬§" in FORMAT tag to "4to" (standard Latin format abbreviation) and "Frantz√Ñ¬®sische" to "Frantzösische"

The XML structure itself was well-formed (proper nesting of ITEM > BIBL > AUTHOR/TITLE/FORMAT/VOLUME/PRICE tags), but contained character encoding corruption throughout (likely from a failed UTF-8/Latin-1 conversion) and a tag name mismatch between PRIZE (opening) and PRICE (closing).
